title: dalbergia lanceolaria var. assamica
intro: Dalbergia lanceolaria var. assamica, also known as the Indian blackwood or Assam blackwood, is a tree species that belongs to the Fabaceae family and is native to the northeastern regions of India and parts of Southeast Asia. This tree is valued for its timber, which is rich in medicinal properties, particularly its bark, leaves, and roots, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The medicinal properties of Dalbergia lanceolaria var. assamica include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which provide health benefits such as reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues. The plant can be used in various herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which are often used in traditional medicine to treat a range of health conditions.
